compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## What

The central index `docs/README.md` is incomplete (4 living/normative docs unlinked) and
mixes living reference docs with historical evidence with no visual/structural
distinction. Complete the index and add a taxonomy — **in place, WITHOUT relocating any
files.**

Verified state (`origin/main`): exactly four `docs/*.md` are unlinked from the index —
`attest-substrate.md` (living developer API ref), `review-policy.md` (normative policy),
`session-id-shims.md` (living reference), and `dco-rollout-evidence.md` (historical). The
other historical docs (`88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`, `bash-migration.md`) ARE already
linked, just not labeled as historical.

## Decision: label-in-place, NO git-mv (resolves the plan-review E2/T7/E4/G1G2 findings)
The audit floated "and/or move historical files under `docs/archive/`". **We deliberately
choose NOT to relocate any file.** Rationale: the inbound-link blast radius of moving these
files is large and cross-cutting — `bash-migration.md` alone is referenced by 8-10 `src/rebar/`
production docstrings, the root `README.md`, `docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md`, and
test files; `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` is referenced by
`infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh`. A `git-mv` would silently break all of these
for near-zero reader benefit. Instead, historical docs stay at their current paths and are
grouped under a clearly-labeled **"Historical evidence"** section in the index. No file
moves ⇒ no inbound link can break.

## Scope
- Add index entries for the 3 living/normative unlinked docs (`attest-substrate.md`,
  `review-policy.md`, `session-id-shims.md`) under the appropriate living-reference/policy
  buckets.
- Add a clearly-labeled **"Historical evidence"** section to the index and list the
  historical docs there (`dco-rollout-evidence.md` — currently unlinked, add it;
  `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` and `bash-migration.md` — move their LISTING into this
  section, files stay put).
- Add a short taxonomy note at the top of the index defining the buckets: **living
  reference / policy / design rationale / historical evidence** — so future docs land in
  the right place.
- Do NOT git-mv or move any file; do NOT edit any inbound links (nothing moves).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The 3 living/normative docs are each linked EXACTLY ONCE from the index
      (verify, prints nothing):
      `for b in attest-substrate.md review-policy.md session-id-shims.md; do [ "$(grep -cF "($b)" docs/README.md)" = 1 ] || echo "BAD $b"; done`
- [ ] The 3 historical docs are each linked EXACTLY ONCE (so the old Contributor
      entries for bash-migration.md / 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md were MOVED, not
      duplicated) AND appear under the "Historical evidence" heading (verify, prints
      nothing for both loops):
      `for b in dco-rollout-evidence.md 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md bash-migration.md; do [ "$(grep -cF "($b)" docs/README.md)" = 1 ] || echo "DUP/MISSING $b"; done`
      and
      `for b in dco-rollout-evidence.md 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md bash-migration.md; do awk '/^## Historical evidence/{h=1;next} /^## /{h=0} h' docs/README.md | grep -qF "($b)" || echo "NOT-IN-SECTION $b"; done`
      (`grep -cF` is a LITERAL count — no regex/dot-escaping issues; `=1` catches both
      a missing link and a duplicate left behind in Contributor, closing the G6 gap.)
- [ ] A "Historical evidence" section heading exists and a brief taxonomy note defines
      the four buckets (verify, prints nothing):
      `grep -qi '^#\+ *Historical evidence' docs/README.md || echo "NO SECTION"; for t in 'living reference' 'policy' 'design rationale' 'historical evidence'; do grep -qi "$t" docs/README.md || echo "NO BUCKET: $t"; done`
- [ ] No file is relocated and no inbound link edited — only `docs/README.md` changes
      (verify): `git status --porcelain -- docs/` shows exactly one ` M docs/README.md`
      line and NO `R`/`D` (rename/delete) entries.

Files: `docs/README.md` (only).

file_impact.reason: add missing living-doc links + Historical evidence section + taxonomy (no file moves)

compiled_state.title: P2: Complete & de-mix docs index; separate living refs from historical evidence
